[[Lelea Paea]] (brother)
[[Eddie Paea]] (cousin) }} }} '''Mickey Paea''' (born 25 March 1986) is a former [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}}. He previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]], [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in two separate spells in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Paea was born in [[Auburn, New South Wales]], Australia. Paea is of [[Demographics of Tonga|Tongan]] descent and played his junior rugby league for the [[Minto Cobras]], before being signed by the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Paea is the younger brother of former [[Sydney Roosters]] players [[Lopini Paea]] and [[Lelea Paea]].{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Mickey-Paea-set-family-affair-Hull-KR-face/story-17370577-detail/story.html|title=Rugby league: Mickey and Lopini Paea to face off in first match of 2013|work=Hull Daily Mail|date=2012-11-20|access-date=2015-05-22}} ==Playing career== ===Sydney Roosters=== In round 15 of the [[2005 NRL season]], Paea made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for the Roosters against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] at [[Endeavour Field|Toyota Park]].{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2005/round-15/cronulla-vs-sydney/summary.html|title=NRL 2005 – Round 15|publisher=Rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2015-05-22}} Although only playing 4 games for the Roosters in 2005, Paea went on to captain the [[Junior Kangaroos]]{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/250123/mickey-paea|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150522084955/http://www.hullfc.com/first-team/profile/250123/mickey-paea|url-status=dead|archive-date=2015-05-22|title=The Official Hull FC Website|publisher=Hullfc.com|date=1986-03-25|access-date=2015-05-22}} and claimed the Junior Rep Player of the Year at the Roosters awards night. In 2006, Paea made his international début for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].{{cite web|url=http://www.samoaobserver.ws/local/LNPages/0308/2808ln013.htm|title=NRL's island talent|publisher=Samoa Observer|date=2008-03-28|access-date=2008-08-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081208154608/http://www.samoaobserver.ws/local/LNPages/0308/2808ln013.htm|archive-date=8 December 2008 }} He played in the 2006 [[NSW Cup]] grand final for [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]] who were the [[Sydney Roosters]] feeder club at the time against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. Newtown would lose the grand final 20-19 at [[Stadium Australia]].{{cite web|url=https://www.newtownjets.com/pastSeasons/2007-news/2006GrandFinal.php/|title=2006 Grand Final - Newtown Jets|website=www.newtownjets.com}} In August 2008, Paea was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Tonga|Tongan training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3935853,00.html|title=Tug-of-war over Mason|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-05|access-date=2008-08-06}} and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man squad.{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-08|access-date=2008-10-09}} ===St. George Illawarra Dragons=== On 16 September 2008, Paea signed a one-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2009.{{cite web|url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=632193|title=Mickey Paea signs NRL deal with Dragons|publisher=Wwos.ninemsn.com.au|access-date=2015-05-22}}{{cite web|url=http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/ |title=2009 NRL Player Movements |publisher=NRL Live |date=2008-10-04 |access-date=2008-10-04 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081005172545/http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/ |archive-date=5 October 2008 }} He made his Dragons début in [[Townsville]], in Round 9 of the [[2009 NRL season]] against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2009/round-9/north-queensland-vs-st-george-illawarra/summary.html|title=NRL 2009 – Round 9|publisher=Rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2015-05-22}} On 23 May 2009, Paea was the subject of a controversial racial vilification dispute with [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] captain, [[Paul Gallen]], late in the local derby played between the Dragons and Sharks. Gallen, who took objection to Paea accidentally stepping on his hand in a tackle, got up and called Paea a "black cunt".{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqnews/paea-urged-to-speak-up-on-racial-slur/2009/05/24/1243103433663.html|title=Paea urged to speak up on racial slur|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2009-05-25|access-date=2009-05-28}} Paea's angry response was caught by television cameras and microphones, but he did not wish to take the matter further after the match.{{cite web|url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-sport/paea-plays-down-gallen-bustup-20090523-bj1h.html|title=Paea plays down Gallen bust-up|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2009-05-23|access-date=2009-05-28}} After the match, Paea said, "But then something told me to just forget about it and to just move on. Things happen on the field and you don't want to get too personal about it. But I've been getting different kinds of reactions. After the game I had my church friends sending me messages saying, 'You handled it really well, it was very Kingdom the way you dealt with it'. Then I had other mates saying, 'You should have smashed him'." Nevertheless, the NRL decided to step in and issued a $10,000 fine to Gallen.{{cite web|url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-sport/gallop-stands-by-gallen-breach-notice-20090525-bkcc.html|title=Gallop stands by Gallen breach notice|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2009-05-25|access-date=2009-05-28}}{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/sport/nrl/story/0,26799,25543937-5006066,00.html|title=Cronulla Sharks to sack skipper Paul Gallen|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=2009-05-27|access-date=2009-05-28}} [[File:mickeypaea.jpg|right|thumb|Paea playing for the Bulldogs]] ===Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs=== On 13 August 2009, Paea signed a two-year contract with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] starting in 2010.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/bulldogs-sign-young-talent/story-e6frexni-1225761026798|title=Bulldogs sign young talent|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-05-22}} He went on to make 16 appearances in 2010 and 12 appearances in 2011.{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NC0tNC0tLS0tLTIxMjAtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2015-05-22}} ===Hull Kingston Rovers=== On 6 October 2011, Paea signed a two-year contract with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] to move to the [[Super League]] starting in 2012.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/15189886|title=BBC Sport – Hull KR sign forward Mickey Paea from Canterbury|publisher=Bbc.com|date=2011-10-06|access-date=2015-05-22}} In October and November, Paea played for Tonga in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=http://www.triplem.com.au/sydney/sport/nrl/news/2013/10/tonga-names-rugby-league-world-cup-2013-squad/|title=Tonga Names Rugby League World Cup 2013 Squad | News|publisher=Triplem.com.au|date=2013-10-08|access-date=2015-05-22}} ===Hull F.C.=== On 28 June 2013, Paea signed a 2-year contract with Hull KR's cross-town rivals [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2014.{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/article/29802/hull-confirm-paea-signing-for-2014|title=The Official Hull FC Website|publisher=Hullfc.com|date=2013-06-28|access-date=2015-05-22}} ===Newcastle Knights=== On 22 May 2015, Paea signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2016, to return to the [[National Rugby League]] after four years in [[England]]. Paea's two seasons at Newcastle saw the club finish with back to back [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]]'s.{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/05/22/knights_announce_pae.html|title=Knights announce Paea signing – Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-22}}{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/12/12/knights-2017-by-the-numbers/|title=Knights: 2017 by the numbers|website=NRL}}{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News}} ===Return to Hull F.C.=== In August 2017, Paea signed a signed a two-year contract with Hull F.C., to return to the [[Super League]] in 2018.{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/hull-fc-sign-nrl-duo-2018-28501/|title=Hull FC sign NRL duo for 2018 – Zero Tackle|date=1 August 2017|access-date=2 August 2017}} ==References== {{reflist}}
